company_description: EliseAI builds AI agents for the housing and healthcare industries, integrating them deeply into existing workflows to make them more efficient, reduce costs, and improve the experience. In housing, EliseAI simplifies how renters tour apartments, sign leases, submit maintenance requests, and stay connected with their property team. In healthcare, it makes it easy to schedule appointments, complete intake forms, and communicate with providers. The company recently raised a $250 million Series E round led by Andreessen Horowitz. This role is on the Future Platforms team, which operates as a startup within a startup focused on executing the next generation of AI products.
company_industry: AI / PropTech / housing and healthcare automation
remote_policy: Onsite (in person 4-5 days a week at NYC office), with willingness to travel ~1x per month for ~3 days
salary_raw: The salary range for this role is $190,000 - $250,000. EliseAI offers a competitive total rewards package which includes base salary, equity, and a comprehensive benefits & perks package. Exact compensation is determined based on a number of factors including experience, skill level, location and qualifications which are assessed during the interview process.
responsibilities: Own engagement success end-to-end: Serve as the primary driver of customer deployments from late-stage sales handoff through launch and post-launch stabilization; ensure outcomes, timelines, and stakeholder alignment.
Support sales and expansion: Participate in later-stage sales and pre-sales discovery to set up successful engagements; help scope realistic engagements and identify follow-on opportunities.

Lead discovery, scoping, and decisioning: Translate fuzzy goals into crisp requirements, workflows, success metrics, and rollout plans; guide customers through tradeoffs, product constraints, and adoption choices.

Run the execution motion: Maintain multi-threaded project plans under ambiguity; coordinate across internal teams and external stakeholders; create clear operating cadences, risk tracking, and launch readiness.
Configuration and launch: Collaborate with product operations, engineering, and training teams to drive product configuration, data migration, launch, and successful adoption.

Inform and support product development: Partner with Product/Engineering to define net-new product workflows and requirements and help shape the product roadmap based on scoping learnings, customer input, and real-world usage.
Build the scalable playbook: Turn lessons from engagements into repeatable processes, templates, and systems improving speed and quality as we scale.
Attract top-tier talent to join our driven team
required_qualifications: 5-8 years of experience in management consulting, corporate strategy, product management, and/or founder/operator roles at high-growth startups
Strong technical intuition, demonstrated by STEM degree or equivalent technical background
Proven ability to take ownership and operate autonomously with strong judgement in fast-paced, ambiguous environments
Proven ability to independently manage complex projects end-to-end in a client facing setting
Excellent communication and ability to naturally build strong relationships and influence across internal and external stakeholders
Ability to operate creatively and flexibly while maintaining sharp attention to detail
Willingness to travel ~1x per month for ~3 days
Willingness to work in person at our office 4-5 days a week
benefits: Medical, Dental and Vision premiums covered at 100%
Fitness & home services stipend to cover part of your expenses
A collaborative in-office environment with an open floor plan, fully stocked kitchen, and all meals covered in the office

company_industry: AI infrastructure / human training data for frontier AI labs
company_description: At Pareto, we build the platform that turns expert human judgment into the data, evals, and RL environments frontier models learn from. We work with leading frontier labs like Anthropic and GDM, and we give skilled people everywhere a way to shape the future of AI and share in what it creates. This RL environment and human-data infrastructure is already in production; the job now is to scale it. Pareto builds human training data pipelines for frontier AI labs.
seniority_level: IC / Lead (technical operations, leads a team of project managers)
salary_raw: $140K – $180K • Offers Equity
responsibilities: Pipeline architecture: Design end-to-end data collection and evaluation pipelines for RLVR, RLHF, SFT, red-teaming, and model evaluation workflows — including expert sampling strategy, annotation schema, rubric structure, inter-rater calibration, and QA system design
Prototype novel workflows quickly, identify architectural risks before launch, and make tradeoff decisions with confidence
Understand how agents interact with tools to solve expert-driven tasks and communicate with engineering to ensure the environment is built accordingly
Agentic system deployment: Build, test, and iterate on AI agents that automate pipeline tasks — quality gate review, expert matching, output flagging, throughput anomaly detection
Work closely with engineering to scope agent capabilities, write prompts and evaluation logic to make agents reliable, and monitor their performance in production

Quality systems: Define data quality standards across annotation, evaluation, and expert output review; design and run audits using inter-rater reliability metrics, calibration sets, and statistical sampling
Build systems that prevent quality issues — automated checks, structured output validation, and model-assisted review layers
Spot-check tasks, understand what makes a datapoint meaningful and high quality, and translate findings into clear feedback and expert guidelines
Client interface: Engage directly with AI researchers, TPMs, and PMs at client organizations; translate research-driven requirements into operational workflows
Communicate pipeline performance clearly, escalate technical risks early, and contribute to project scoping and pricing decisions
Research integration: Stay current with LLM post-training, evaluation methodology, and data tooling; evaluate and integrate new approaches into active pipelines
Own delivery against model performance benchmarks and lead a team of project managers who handle day-to-day execution tracking
required_qualifications: Proficiency in Python and SQL for data manipulation, pipeline monitoring, and quality analysis — comfortable writing light scripts to parse formats, run statistical checks, and build lightweight tooling
Working knowledge of LLM internals: RLHF/SFT training loops, how prompt structure affects output distribution, RL environment setup qualities (tool use) for agentic data collection / eval projects
Hands-on experience with at least one agentic or LLM workflow framework (LangChain, DSPy, AutoGen, direct tool-use via API, or equivalent)
Demonstrated ownership of a data or ML pipeline from scoping through delivery — including quality design, not just throughput tracking
Strong written communication: ability to write technical guidelines and rubrics that distributed expert workers follow accurately, and brief senior researchers on pipeline performance
Comfort operating with ambiguity in a fast-moving environment where model requirements shift and client priorities evolve
preferred_qualifications: Direct experience with RL environment data pipelines, evaluation framework design, and red-teaming workflows
Background in data engineering, ML research support or equivalent
Experience designing or operating agentic systems in a production or near-production context
Familiarity with inter-rater reliability methods, calibration set design, and annotation quality frameworks
Prior client-facing or technical program management experience in an AI/ML-adjacent context
Prior experience scoping or driving projects with fuzzy upfront specs or evolving requirements; a high-ownership position where you take charge and lead, not simply follow project guidelines

seniority_level: Chief of Staff / senior operator (reports to VP of Care Transformation)
salary_raw: We offer a base compensation range of approximately $175,000–$233,00 per year, exclusive of equity. This intentionally broad range provides flexibility for candidates to tailor their cash and equity mix based on individual preferences. Our compensation philosophy prioritizes meaningful equity grants, enabling team members to share directly in the impact they help create.
responsibilities: Run the CT operating system: own the meeting cadence, agenda, decision log, and follow-through (leadership syncs, all-hands, account reviews); set the rhythm, prep materials, and ensure the right people have the right information so nothing falls through the cracks

Give CT leadership real-time visibility: build and maintain dashboards, trackers, and weekly briefs showing what's green, at-risk, and needs attention; own the CT headcount model, track the budget, and translate account pipeline into staffing plans

Own OKRs and improvement initiatives: track progress against CT OKRs, surface blockers before they compound, drive quarterly retrospectives, and own the inventory of improvement initiatives from inception through completion

Be the bridge between CT and Marketing: build the operating rhythm so CT teams receive information before facing customers, own a 4-stream sequencing model (company vision, product narrative, feature update, release note), and build/maintain the centralized CT enablement hub (messaging, pitches, battle cards, competitive positioning, product context); first deliverable is a gold-standard inpatient pitch

Anticipate needs before they become urgent (QBR narratives, renewal messaging, product change explanations); within 30 days establish a mechanism for CT to surface upcoming needs to Marketing, and within 60 days demonstrate addressing at least two needs before being asked
Own major moments: CT's operational contribution to HFMA Annual Conference (June 2026), ViVE, and similar events, including customer speaker prep, CTE briefing, and account-level message alignment
required_qualifications: 5-8 years in roles requiring both operating rigor and content/enablement judgment (strategy & ops at a high-growth B2B company, customer success operations, RevOps, or enablement leadership in healthcare tech); strong in both dimensions, not just one
Has built something from scratch before (a team function, information system, or operating cadence) in an environment that didn't hand it to you
Familiar with health system sales cycles and the dynamics of enterprise post-sale relationships (understands differences between a large academic medical center and a regional IDN)
Strong written communicator able to translate complex product updates into crisp, audience-specific briefs (knowing what a CSM vs. a CTE vs. a support team needs to hear)
Operationally literate: comfortable owning a dashboard, budget tracker, planning model, and agenda simultaneously without needing work checked
Moves work forward through clarity and trust rather than positional authority; influence without authority is the default mode
Low ego and high follow-through: sends the recap, tracks open items, closes the loop without being asked; comfortable in executive conversations and in unglamorous infrastructure work
Thrives in ambiguity and starts writing the playbook before it exists
benefits: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents
401(k) with a company match of up to 3% of base salary
Remote-friendly culture (with a San Francisco HQ) and full equipment provisioning
Annual company-wide off-sites, team off-sites, and regular team lunches and all-hands gatherings, with travel, lodging, and meals covered
Flexible time off with no annual cap, company-wide holidays, and an annual holiday shutdown from December 24-January 1

AI infrastructure / AI for science (life sciences, drug discovery, materials science, financial services, navigation, cybersecurity)
company_description: SandboxAQ is a high-growth company delivering AI solutions that address some of the world's greatest challenges. The company's Large Quantitative Models (LQMs) power advances in life sciences, financial services, navigation, cybersecurity, and other sectors. It is a global, tech-focused team of experts in AI, chemistry, cybersecurity, physics, mathematics, medicine, and engineering. The company emerged from Alphabet Inc. as an independent, growth capital-backed company in 2022, funded by leading investors and supported by a braintrust of industry leaders.
salary_raw: $195.2K – $366K • Multiple Ranges (US Tier 1: $244K – $366K; US Tier 2: $219.6K – $329.4K; US Tier 3: $195.2K – $292.8K)
responsibilities: Strategic Business Architect: Provide operational support and leadership to BioSim and ChemSim divisions, running day-to-day business operations including identifying synergies in resource allocation and operationalizing scaling systems; influence and partner with Engineering, Science, and GTM.

Rigorous Resourcing & Capacity Management: Lead the transition from high-growth experimentation to scaled operations by optimizing talent density and capacity across PhDs, SWEs, and GTM specialists; architect dynamic headcount models aligned with technical milestones and vertical-specific revenue triggers; accountable for multi-million dollar compute budgets; introduce and manage telemetry around human resource capacity allocation across AI Simulation.

Tactical Execution: Responsible for hands-on, end-to-end execution; track velocity across complex milestones; rapidly shift financial and talent resources as requirements arise; establish standards for tracking milestones, manage tradeoffs in resource allocation, and communicate effectively across leadership.

Interim Leadership & Chemical Simulation Oversight: Spearhead the Chemical Simulation business unit during expansion; swiftly establish and operationalize high-priority verticals including Batteries, Catalysis Discovery, Semiconductor Substrates, Metal Alloys, and Magnets; define the playbook for resourcing and scaling these initiatives and identify synergies across ChemSim and BioSim.

Service Leadership & Organizational Health: Lead by influence with partners in Engineering, R&D, Science, GTM, and HR; use forensic root-cause analysis and high EQ to diagnose friction, resolve organizational debt, and keep team culture high-performing and mission-aligned during rapid scaling; navigate friction and facilitate difficult conversations with candor and empathy.

Financial & Technical Stewardship: Manage rigorous trade-off analysis for high-stakes technical investments, ensure resource allocation is synchronized between divisions and aligned with global revenue triggers; diagnose bottlenecks and stuck workflows and implement structural fixes; implement frontier AI and automation technologies to streamline internal workflows, reporting, and revenue tracking.
required_qualifications: COO Pedigree: Deep experience in operational leadership, including tenure as a COO, Managing Director, or Head of Ops at venture-backed scaled startups; proven track record of scaling companies from Series B through to mature operations or successful exits.
Technical Fluency: Deep familiarity with AI or heavy-compute environments; ability to translate the needs of Research Scientists and Executives into tactical execution against business requirements.
Tactical Mastery: A 'Builder-Operator' mindset with the ability to pivot instantly from deep-dive spreadsheet/Jira analysis to board-level strategic planning.
Forensic Problem Solving: Proven expertise in conducting root-cause analysis on both operational delivery failures and human dynamics (attrition, team friction) within multidisciplinary teams.
preferred_qualifications: Experience setting up and scaling new business verticals or startup-within-a-startup units.
A track record of implementing AI/LLM tools to automate internal business functions.
Experience managing global, remote-first teams through significant organizational change.
Proven experience navigating complex B2B sales cycles and technical partnerships with Hyperscalers or global industrial leaders.

Familiarity with Life Sciences (Drug Discovery) or Materials Science (battery, semiconductor, or metallurgy sectors) and/or direct experience managing programs where the US Government was a primary customer, particularly regarding dual-use technology, national security R&D, or government contracting frameworks.
benefits: Competitive base salary, performance-based incentives or bonuses (where applicable), and equity participation
Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for employees and dependents with generous employer premium contributions
Paid parental leave and inclusive family-building benefits
Flexible paid time off and company-wide seasonal breaks
Career development: continuous learning and growth, cross-functional collaboration, and access to internal learning and development programs

company_description: Handshake was founded on a simple belief that everyone deserves a path to a great career, regardless of where they went to school or who they know. Today, we power 25 million job seekers, 1 million+ employers, and 1,600 educational institutions. In 2025, the company started Handshake AI, working directly with frontier AI lab researchers to create evaluations, publish benchmarks, and push the boundary of data; it has grown from $0 to ~$1B run rate and pays ~$60M to over 30K individuals every month. Human data is positioned as core infrastructure for AI advancement, and Handshake AI supports frontier AI labs on their most complex data at the largest scale.
company_industry: AI data / career platform (two-sided marketplace; AI infra and HR tech)
seniority_level: Senior IC / executive support (Chief of Staff to CEO)
salary_raw: competitive compensation
responsibilities: Drive the CEO's operating rhythm — from board prep and executive staff meetings to annual planning — so decisions get made and priorities stay clear across the company
Lead high-stakes cross-functional initiatives (GTM alignment, org design, IPO readiness) from ambiguous idea to structured execution with clear owners and measurable outcomes
Build the analytical foundation behind key decisions by interrogating complex datasets, modeling financials, and distilling insights into crisp, actionable recommendations for the CEO and board
Produce world-class communications — board decks, investor memos, all-hands narratives — that represent the CEO's thinking with precision and reflect Handshake's strategy externally
required_qualifications: 8+ years in consulting, strategy & operations, investment banking, or a senior operating role at a technology company
Experience inside scaled organizations (500+ employees), with exposure to the complexity of late-stage growth — board dynamics, cross-functional alignment, and high-stakes external relationships
Exceptional analytical ability: you build the analytical foundation that shapes exec-level decisions, including financial models and business cases that influence board-level strategy (SQL a plus)
Elite written communication skills — board-quality decks, executive memos, and all-hands messaging that don't need much editing
High bias to action and low ego: you anticipate needs, act before being asked, and measure success entirely by the outcomes of the people you support
Track record of influencing senior leaders and driving alignment across an executive team without direct authority, in fast-moving, ambiguous environments
Track record of driving complex, cross-functional projects from ambiguity to execution in fast-moving environments
preferred_qualifications: Background in business operations, strategy & operations, or strategic finance — with experience moving from insight to execution across a fast-moving org
Experience operating across company stages — from early-stage, high-growth environments to scaled organizations — and the ability to adapt your operating style accordingly
Familiarity with marketplace businesses, multi-sided platforms, or AI/SaaS go-to-market motions
benefits: Ownership: Equity in a fast-growing company
Financial Wellness: 401(k) match, competitive compensation, financial coaching
Family Support: Paid parental leave, fertility benefits, parental coaching
Wellbeing: Medical, dental, and vision, mental health support, $500 wellness stipend
Remote & Office: Internet, commuting, and free lunch/gym in SF office
Time Off: Flexible PTO, 15 holidays + 2 flex days
